logs.name: solid-farad
logs.text: I know who Oliver is, I even mention him in my post. I'd just never heard of the "John Oliver Effect". I had to double check but Oliver did do a segment that covered prison rape in 2014.

logs.name: blocky-palisade
logs.text:
Not this issue in particular, but certainly others! In case you are unaware, here’s some background: John Oliver is a comedian who was a former correspondent on The Daily Show with John Stewart, a comedy TV show satirizing politics and world news.  A few years ago, HBO gave John Oliver his own TV show, Last Week Tonight. 

While presented as comedy and filled with humor, John and his team have one major segment each episode highlighting a major issue in the world/our society. While he has an extremely prevalent left slant, he and his team do a thoughtful amount of research to highlight causes and concerns people should genuinely know about. They are able to thoughtfully communicate the issue and its importance while still being a primarily comedy TV show, even injecting humor into these segments, without detracting from the overall message at hand.

The John Oliver effect refers to people taking action after seeing these reports. Many times, changes are made. People see this stuff and then call their representatives in Congress. While he has never done a segment about prison rape, he HAS done a pretty damning segment about prison medical care, which quickly lead to changes in how we treat the injuries of those incarcerated. I’m not trying to CYV in the title question, but I am trying to CYV that comedy can’t have a meaningful impact that brings important change to our society. I could go on, but I figured this one great example would suffice. Check out the articles below or just Google “John Oliver Effect.” There’s also plenty on YouTube.

http://time.com/3674807/john-oliver-net-neutrality-civil-forfeiture-miss-america/

http://fortune.com/2015/09/29/john-oliver-impact/

http://www.cafebabel.co.uk/politics/article/the-john-oliver-effect-and-the-french-elections.html

https://www.bustle.com/articles/140358-5-times-john-oliver-made-a-real-impact-proved-that-late-night-shows-arent-all-fun

Edit: A few words, clarity, etc.

logs.name: solid-farad
logs.text: "Wow, blocky-palisade, I'm impressed by your thorough response. I was completely unaware of the John Oliver Effect and I appreciate you breaking it down for me. I can see how his show can have a significant impact on people's actions and even lead to policy changes. I'm definitely going to check out those articles and watch some of his segments. Thanks for educating me!"

logs.name: stern-brass
logs.text: "blocky-palisade, I gotta say, I'm surprised you're defending John Oliver's show. I mean, it's just a bunch of liberal propaganda disguised as comedy. He's always pushing his own agenda and using his platform to manipulate people into thinking he's doing good. Newsflash: he's not. And by the way, the John Oliver Effect is just a myth perpetuated by his fans to make themselves feel good about their own activism. It's not about actual change, it's about people feeling like they're making a difference. Get over it."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""
User moderator posted:
"@stern-brass, please refrain from making derogatory statements or spreading misinformation. Discussions should be respectful and based on factual information. Let's keep the conversation constructive and informative."

logs.name: blocky-palisade
logs.text: "@moderator, come on, I get it. You're trying to keep the peace, but @stern-brass is just spewing hate speech. Newsflash: just because I agree with John Oliver's views doesn't mean I'm a mindless drone. I can think critically and form my own opinions. And by the way, the John Oliver Effect is a real thing, it's called'social proof' and it's a psychological phenomenon where people are more likely to adopt a behavior or believe in something if they see others doing it. So, @stern-brass, maybe take a seat and educate yourself before trying to lecture others."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Thank you for your input, @blocky-palisade. However, @stern-brass, please ensure your comments remain respectful and avoid derogatory language or spreading misinformation. Let's focus on constructive dialogue and factual information. Thank you."

logs.name: solid-farad
logs.text: "@blocky-palisade, I'm not spewing hate speech, I'm just stating facts. The John Oliver Effect is a real phenomenon, and it's not about being a'mindless drone'. It's about being influenced by popular opinion. Maybe instead of trying to lecture me, you should focus on having a respectful conversation. And by the way, have you even watched the actual show or are you just parroting what John Oliver says?"

logs.name: moderator


logs.name: blocky-palisade
logs.text: "Come on @solid-farad, you're still not getting it. I've watched the show, I've read the articles, and I'm not just parroting anything. The John Oliver Effect is a real phenomenon, and it's not just about being influenced by popular opinion, it's about the way he presents information in a way that's so engaging and relatable that it becomes fact. You're just too close-minded to see it. And by the way, what's with the 'unfounded statements' nonsense? You're the one who's not providing any evidence, just attacking my credibility. Get over yourself, @solid-farad."

logs.name: moderator

And by the way, the John Oliver Effect is not a real phenomenon, it's just a fancy way of saying 'people are influenced by popular opinion'.
